problem_statement: A documentation portal's visual qualification gate reports widespread page-layout changes even though the product source and captured page inputs are held constant. The stored comparison records were produced a month apart, most differences disappear under a partially reconstructed environment, and the original comparison environment is no longer available. Determine the defensible disposition and the next measurement needed before blocking or changing the product.

observations.statement: The qualification report flags 184 of 620 screenshots, concentrated in text wrapping, line height, and glyph width rather than missing content or failed requests.
The stored reference record for the portal home page used the listed code and input fingerprints and produced outcome ref-44a9 in environment runner-june-browser125-fontset-a.
The candidate record for the same comparison key holds the code and input fingerprints unchanged but produces outcome cand-b71e in environment runner-july-browser126-fontset-b.

Reference metadata preserved only a floating runner tag; registry retention shows that tag now resolves to the July image, and neither the June image digest nor its complete font inventory was recorded.
observations.provenance: runner registry retention report and metadata audit

observations.statement: An offline replay using the current page bundle with a reconstructed browser-125 and June font set removes 181 of the 184 flagged differences, while three small glyph-boundary differences remain and the reconstruction cannot be proven identical to the original runner.
Two clean reruns in the fully pinned July environment produce identical screenshot hashes for all 620 pages, arguing against ordinary run-to-run nondeterminism in that environment.
For the 184 flagged pages, captured HTML, API-response, stylesheet, and web-font response hashes match between the two stored runs; only raster outputs and their environment records differ.
Synthetic layout telemetry from the deployed portal and sampled user sessions shows no step change in overflow, layout-shift, or font-load failure rates during the qualification window.

The comparison service did not record browser build, font-file checksums, device-scale rounding mode, or image digest as first-class fields in June, so no retained artifact can isolate which environmental component accounts for the remaining three differences.
observations.provenance: comparison record schema and June artifact inventory
